pi_items.verified_value: StatCalc is a statistical calculator module in Epi Info 7
StatCalc includes a Tables (2x2) calculator for 2x2 contingency table analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: StatCalc will automatically calculate and display the odds ratio
pi_items.verified_value: StatCalc outputs odds ratios and risk ratios with confidence limits

pi_items.metadata_value: StatCalc will automatically calculate and display the 95% confidence interval
pi_items.verified_value: StatCalc outputs odds ratios and risk ratios with 95% confidence limits (Lower and Upper bounds)

pi_items.metadata_value: StatCalc will automatically calculate and display the chi-square p-value
pi_items.verified_value: StatCalc outputs chi-square tests (several types) and associated p-values
Input value 20 (Exposed+Ill) is mathematically consistent with 30 total exposed and 10 not ill
pi_items.source: Task description states both '20 of 30 exposed' and 'Exposed+Not Ill=10', which sum to 30

pi_items.verified_value: Input value 5 (Unexposed+Ill) is mathematically consistent with 25 total unexposed and 20 not ill
pi_items.source: Task description states both '5 of 25 unexposed' and 'Unexposed+Not Ill=20', which sum to 25

pi_items.metadata_value: Agent should note the odds ratio value from output (not explicitly stated)
pi_items.source: Standard odds ratio formula: OR = (a×d)/(b×c). Verified via multiple epidemiological statistics sources (NCBI, CDC documentation)

pi_items.verified_value: Realistic epidemiological case scenario appropriate for training StatCalc usage
pi_items.source: CDC conducts actual foodborne illness outbreak investigations (https://www.cdc.gov/foodborne-outbreaks/). This task uses a hypothetical but realistic scenario

privileged_info_summary: Epi Info 7 is real CDC epidemiology software with a StatCalc module containing a Tables (2x2) calculator for contingency table analysis. StatCalc calculates odds ratios with 95% confidence intervals, chi-square tests, and p-values. The task uses a synthetic foodborne illness outbreak scenario with consistent input data: 20 exposed+ill, 10 exposed+not ill, 5 unexposed+ill, 20 unexposed+not ill. These values yield an odds ratio of 8.0, indicating a strong association between exposure and illness. All claimed software features, output statistics, and mathematical consistency are verified.
